name: Reaction
domain: Chemical kinetics, catalysis, rate laws, autocatalysis
core_insight: Reactions proceed when molecules have enough energy to cross the activation barrier. Temperature is the master accelerant. Autocatalytic reactions are self-amplifying — the product catalyzes its own formation, creating explosive growth from small beginnings.

key_equations.name: Arrhenius Equation
key_equations.why: Rate constant as function of temperature: activation energy and thermal fluctuations compete

key_equations.name: Mass Action Law
key_equations.why: Rate proportional to reactant concentrations raised to their order — the kinetics fingerprint

key_equations.name: Autocatalytic Reaction
key_equations.why: Product catalyzes itself: exponential amplification, the chemical basis of life's self-replication

seed_prompt: ⇑ What is the activation barrier? At what temperature does this ignite? Is the product self-catalytic?

fourth_equation.name: Transition State Theory
fourth_equation.why: Rate constant from free energy of activation — the Boltzmann factor weighing the probability of reaching the transition state

explore_paths.question: Where does chemistry end and life begin?
explore_paths.why: Autocatalytic reaction networks are the chemical precondition for self-replication — the boundary between F13 and F04 is the origin of life

explore_paths.question: How does temperature set the pace of all reactions?
explore_paths.why: The Arrhenius equation is a thermodynamic result — activation energy and thermal fluctuations (kT) are F05 quantities governing F13 rates

explore_paths.question: Can reaction networks compute?
explore_paths.why: Chemical reaction networks can implement Boolean logic and even universal computation — living cells compute via reaction networks

explore_paths.question: What catalysts might transform what is chemically possible?
explore_paths.why: Catalysis is transformation without transformation — the catalyst lowers the barrier without itself being transformed; a living instance of P11

open_questions.question: Did life begin with RNA first (RNA world), metabolism first, or something else entirely?
open_questions.tension: RNA world (Crick, Orgel): RNA both stores information and catalyzes reactions — the first self-replicating molecule. Metabolism first (Wächtershäuser, Russell): proto-metabolic cycles on mineral surfaces created the chemical preconditions before any replicator. Lipid world: membrane compartments came first. No laboratory has successfully created self-replicating RNA from scratch under plausible prebiotic conditions.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: This is the origin of life question dressed in chemistry. Every proposed solution has a 'chicken-and-egg' problem. RNA needs proteins to replicate efficiently; proteins need RNA to be encoded. The first autocatalytic set had to solve this without any of the existing machinery.

open_questions.question: Can artificial protocells (synthetic minimal cells) be built from scratch?
open_questions.tension: The Venter Institute synthesized a cell with a minimal genome (JCVI-syn3.0, 473 genes). But this used an existing cell chassis — the cytoplasm was provided. Building a cell purely from defined chemical components in a lipid vesicle with self-replication has not been achieved. The gap between having all the parts and having a working system is the fundamental challenge.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If we can build a cell, we understand life. If we cannot, we are missing something fundamental. The attempt itself has revealed how many 'simple' components turn out to be irreducibly complex systems.

open_questions.question: Is there a universal mechanism for autocatalytic set formation, or is it chemistry-specific?
open_questions.tension: Kauffman's autocatalytic sets theory proposes that above a critical complexity threshold, a set of molecules will spontaneously form a self-catalyzing network. Criticisms: the probability estimates assume random catalysis, which may not apply to real chemistry. Some experimental autocatalytic sets have been found, but whether they scale is unknown.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If Kauffman is right, life's origin was not improbable but inevitable above a chemical complexity threshold — the universe is literally autocatalytic. The stakes of this question are enormous.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: The Haber-Bosch process (nitrogen fixation) feeds half the world's population — arguably the most impactful reaction network in human history
cross_domain_bridges.example: Without the iron catalyst in Haber-Bosch, Earth could support maybe 4 billion people; the catalyst is civilization

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Drug action is reaction kinetics — binding, inhibition, and catalysis of biological reaction networks
cross_domain_bridges.example: Enzyme inhibitors (aspirin, statins, ACE inhibitors) work by competing with substrate for the active site or blocking it allosterically

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Ozone depletion and greenhouse gas cycles are global-scale reaction networks with their own kinetics and catalysts
cross_domain_bridges.example: CFCs catalyze ozone destruction with rate constants amplified by polar stratospheric clouds — a tiny amount does enormous damage

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Neurotransmitter synthesis, release, and degradation are reaction kinetics problems; drug action on synaptic chemistry is F13 applied to the brain
cross_domain_bridges.example: SSRIs inhibit serotonin reuptake by blocking the transporter protein — reaction kinetics of mood

noise_signature.pattern: Runaway reaction — temperature rises faster than heat can be removed
noise_signature.diagnosis: Positive thermal feedback; Arrhenius acceleration is outpacing cooling; risk of explosion or decomposition

noise_signature.pattern: Inhibition plateau — reaction rate drops far below expected maximum
noise_signature.diagnosis: Product inhibition, substrate depletion, or catalyst poisoning; the system has accumulated a stopper

noise_signature.pattern: Induction period — long delay before reaction starts despite conditions being met
noise_signature.diagnosis: Chain reaction: requires a critical concentration of initiating species; below the threshold, nothing happens; above it, rapid ignition
